narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with a potent offensive engine, anchored by Pete Crow-Armstrong, who commands the highest projected output (104.88) against Mikolas. His elite BvP trend (+15%) paired with a favorable platoon advantage makes him the cornerstone of today's optimal construction. Trea Turner (87.86) and Colt Keith (80.06) also present high-upside profiles, bolstered by favorable top-of-the-order hitting positions and significant platoon boosts. Kazuma Okamoto rounds out the core, leveraging a strong power profile to capitalize on his matchup against Springs.

Conversely, the team must navigate a volatile spot for Yordan Alvarez (48.20) and Logan O'Hoppe (46.42). Alvarez is suffering from a significant SP skill penalty (-23%) and a neutral-to-negative platoon split against Detmers, requiring careful monitoring for late-game defensive substitutions. While Caleb Durbin carries a minor BvP red flag (-5% over a limited sample), the overall roster efficiency remains high. We will prioritize the heavy platoon advantages for our middle-of-the-order threats to maximize our 5x5 Roto category yields, keeping a close watch on weather-related park factor shifts that could further suppress output in the colder venues.
